qa_pairs.question: How do I enable the automatic resubmission of failed farm cases in the META-Farm package?
qa_pairs.answer: To enable automatic resubmission of failed cases in META-Farm, add the `-auto` switch to your `submit.run` or `resubmit.run` command. This submits an additional serial job that automatically runs `resubmit.run` after the current farm finishes. You must ensure the `resubmit_script.sh` file is present in your farm directory with the correct account name in its `#SBATCH -A` line. Also, the `NJOBS_MAX` parameter in your `config.h` file should be set to at least one less than the maximum number of jobs you can submit on the cluster, to account for this additional job.

qa_pairs.question: How can I configure the META-Farm package to run a post-processing job automatically after all cases are successfully completed?
qa_pairs.answer: To configure an automatic post-processing job, create a script named `final.sh` inside your farm directory. This job will only run automatically if all cases from `table.dat` have been successfully processed (i.e., had a zero exit status). This feature also utilizes the `resubmit_script.sh` script, so ensure it has the correct account name in the `#SBATCH -A` line. You should adjust the `NJOBS_MAX` parameter in `config.h` accordingly; if both auto-resubmission and auto-post-processing are enabled, they will together only submit one additional job.

qa_pairs.question: What is the `WHOLE_NODE` mode in the META-Farm package, and how is it enabled?
qa_pairs.answer: The `WHOLE_NODE` mode in META-Farm allows individual serial farming jobs to be packaged into whole node jobs, making it suitable for clusters like Trillium. This mode is off by default. To enable it, edit the `config.h` file in your farm directory, setting `WHOLE_NODE=1` and `NWHOLE` to the number of CPU cores per node (e.g., 192 for Trillium). When `WHOLE_NODE` mode is enabled, the positive integer argument for the `submit.run` command changes its meaning from the number of meta-jobs to the number of whole nodes to be used.
